text:
REGIME — Small caps led, defensives bid, consumer discretionary lagged.

INDICES — VIX 23.9, falling. IWM +0.69%, QQQ +0.11%, SPY +0.09%, DIA -0.09%. Small caps outperformed large caps across the board. WTI Oil via USO surged +11.15%, the most notable cross-asset move of the session.

SECTORS — XLRE +1.61%, XLK +0.80%, XLP +0.53%, XLU +0.50%, XLE +0.47%, XLC +0.41%, XLF +0.18%, XLB -0.10%, XLI -0.40%, XLV -0.62%, XLY -1.50%. Top industries: USO +11.15%, MSOS +4.62%; bottom industries: TAN -2.52%, SLV -3.45%.

THE 7s — Toll Road +1.64%, Semi +1.27%, Energy +0.91%, New Metals +0.87%, New Staples +0.83%, Cloud +0.75%, Defense +0.55%, Software +0.54%, Old Staples +0.50%, Insurance +0.31%, Buildout +0.19%, Freight +0.18%, Bank +0.15%, Power +0.04%, Medtech +0.00%, Old Metals -0.23%, Housing -0.71%, Health -0.76%, Industrial -0.92%, Fab 7 -1.21%. Strongest ticker in Toll Road undisclosed in data; weakest in Fab 7 dragged the basket to -1.21%.

SIGNALS — Fear & Greed 51.2, Neutral. Top movers: USO +11.15% (Cross-Asset), MSOS +4.62% (Industries), SLV -3.45% (Cross-Asset). 20d momentum: best sector XLE +5.59%, worst XLY -7.02%; best basket Energy +8.04%, worst Software -8.35%. USO's +11.15% single-day spike aligns with Brent Crude at $121.88, a hawkish macro signal amplifying energy sector demand.

WATCH — USO: +11.15%, Brent at $121.88 raises follow-through vs. mean-reversion risk tomorrow. IJT: +0.53%, vol spike 2.6x average warrants monitoring for small-cap momentum confirmation. XLY: -1.50%, worst sector with -7.02% 20d trend; watch for further consumer breakdown if sentiment at 56.6 deteriorates.
